title: Define lichend DAO admission policy
description: Choose and specify how the production border router durably admits DAO origin identities. Announce TOFU pinning alone is insufficient and automatic first-seen admission would create a new security policy. Define operator provisioning, trust upgrade/revocation, persistence, and failure behavior before lichend activates the RPL root.
acceptance_criteria: A reviewed policy identifies admission authority, durable record format, provisioning and revocation flows, restart behavior, and fail-closed handling; implementation-ready acceptance vectors are defined.
notes: Policy defined: Admission authority is the lichend operator via config or admin interface. Durable format uses existing RedundantValue/DAO_ADMISSION_KEYS scoped to (root_addr, instance_id, dodag_id) storing admitted 32B pubkeys. Provisioning: explicit operator list (no auto-TOFU admit for DAO); call admit_dao_origin after pinning. Revocation: remove from persistent set (requires retirement API extension). Restart: open() restores admitted set or fails-closed. Fail-closed on corrupt/persistence error prevents RPL root activation. Acceptance vectors: extend dao_origin_signature.json with admission cases. See spec/06-security.md for trust model alignment.
